Name: UWidgetComponent
Description:
The widget component provides a surface in the 3D environment on which to render widgets normally rendered to the screen.
  Widgets are first rendered to a render target, then that render target is displayed in the world.
 
  Material Properties set by this component on whatever material overrides the default.
  SlateUI [Texture]
  BackColor [Vector]
  TintColorAndOpacity [Vector]
  OpacityFromTexture [Scalar]

Variables.Name: Space
Variables.Description: The coordinate space in which to render the widget

Variables.Name: TimingPolicy
Variables.Description: How this widget should deal with timing, pausing, etc.

Variables.Name: WidgetClass
Variables.Description: The class of User Widget to create and display an instance of

Variables.Name: DrawSize
Variables.Description: The size of the displayed quad.

Variables.Name: bManuallyRedraw
Variables.Description: Should we wait to be told to redraw to actually draw?

Variables.Name: bCheckLowDeviceQualityLevel
Variables.Description: Should LowDevice Phone draw UI?

Variables.Name: bRedrawRequested
Variables.Description: Has anyone requested we redraw?

Variables.Name: RedrawTime
Variables.Description:
The time in between draws, if 0 - we would redraw every frame.  If 1, we would redraw every second.
	  This will work with bManuallyRedraw as well.  So you can say, manually redraw, but only redraw at this
	  maximum rate.

Variables.Name: CurrentDrawSize
Variables.Description:
The actual draw size, this changes based on DrawSize - or the desired size of the widget if
	  bDrawAtDesiredSize is true.

Variables.Name: bDrawAtDesiredSize
Variables.Description:
Causes the render target to automatically match the desired size.
	 
	  WARNING: If you change this every frame, it will be very expensive.  If you need
	     that effect, you should keep the outer widget's sized locked and dynamically
	     scale or resize some inner widget.

Variables.Name: Pivot
Variables.Description: The AlignmentPivot point that the widget is placed at relative to the position.

Variables.Name: bReceiveHardwareInput
Variables.Description:
Register with the viewport for hardware input from the true mouse and keyboard.  These widgets
	  will more or less react like regular 2D widgets in the viewport, e.g. they can and will steal focus
	  from the viewport.
	 
	  WARNING: If you are making a VR game, definitely do not change this to true.  This option should ONLY be used
	  if you're making what would otherwise be a normal menu for a game, just in 3D.  If you also need the game to
	  remain responsive and for the player to be able to interact with UI and move around the world (such as a keypad on a door),
	  use the WidgetInteractionComponent instead.

Variables.Name: bWindowFocusable
Variables.Description: Is the virtual window created to host the widget focusable?

Variables.Name: OwnerPlayer
Variables.Description:
The owner player for a widget component, if this widget is drawn on the screen, this controls
	  what player's screen it appears on for split screen, if not set, users player 0.

Variables.Name: BackgroundColor
Variables.Description: The background color of the component

Variables.Name: TintColorAndOpacity
Variables.Description: Tint color and opacity for this component

Variables.Name: OpacityFromTexture
Variables.Description: Sets the amount of opacity from the widget's UI texture to use when rendering the translucent or masked UI to the viewport (0.0-1.0)

Variables.Name: BlendMode
Variables.Description: The blend mode for the widget.

Variables.Name: bIsTwoSided
Variables.Description: Is the component visible from behind?

Variables.Name: TickWhenOffscreen
Variables.Description: Should the component tick the widget when it's off screen?

Variables.Name: Widget
Variables.Description: The User Widget object displayed and managed by this component

Variables.Name: BodySetup
Variables.Description: The body setup of the displayed quad

Variables.Name: TranslucentMaterial
Variables.Description: The material instance for translucent widget components

Variables.Name: TranslucentMaterial_OneSided
Variables.Description: The material instance for translucent, one-sided widget components

Variables.Name: OpaqueMaterial
Variables.Description: The material instance for opaque widget components

Variables.Name: OpaqueMaterial_OneSided
Variables.Description: The material instance for opaque, one-sided widget components

Variables.Name: MaskedMaterial
Variables.Description: The material instance for masked widget components.

Variables.Name: MaskedMaterial_OneSided
Variables.Description: The material instance for masked, one-sided widget components.

Variables.Name: RenderTarget
Variables.Description: The target to which the user widget is rendered

Variables.Name: MaterialInstance
Variables.Description: The dynamic instance of the material that the render target is attached to

Variables.Name: bEditTimeUsable
Variables.Description: Allows the widget component to be used at editor time.  For use in the VR-Editor.

Variables.Name: SharedLayerName
Variables.Description: Layer Name the widget will live on

Variables.Name: LayerZOrder
Variables.Description: ZOrder the layer will be created on, note this only matters on the first time a new layer is created, subsequent additions to the same layer will use the initially defined ZOrder

Variables.Name: GeometryMode
Variables.Description: Controls the geometry of the widget component. See EWidgetGeometryMode.

Variables.Name: CylinderArcAngle
Variables.Description: Curvature of a cylindrical widget in degrees.

Variables.Name: FlipVector
Variables.Description: Curvature of a cylindrical widget in degrees.

Variables.Name: bUseBackColorInTwoSideMode
Variables.Description: For Two side Color

Variables.Name: bHideIfOccluded
Variables.Description: Hide widget component when the attached parent is occluded in player's view (ONLY VALID IN SCREEN SPACE)

Return.Description: The user widget object displayed by this component
The render target to which the user widget is rendered
The dynamic material instance used to render the user widget

Functions.Name: SetWidget
Functions.Description:
Sets the widget to use directly. This function will keep track of the widget till the next time it's called
	 	with either a newer widget or a nullptr

Functions.Name: SetOwnerPlayer
Functions.Description:
Sets the local player that owns this widget component.  Setting the owning player controls
	  which player's viewport the widget appears on in a split screen scenario.  Additionally it
	  forwards the owning player to the actual UserWidget that is spawned.

Functions.Name: GetOwnerPlayer
Functions.Description: Gets the local player that owns this widget component.

Return.Description: The draw size of the quad in the world

Functions.Name: GetCurrentDrawSize
Functions.Description: Returns the "actual" draw size of the quad in the world

Functions.Name: SetDrawSize
Functions.Description: Sets the draw size of the quad in the world

Functions.Name: RequestRedraw
Functions.Description: Requests that the widget be redrawn.

Functions.Name: GetTickWhenOffscreen
Functions.Description: Gets whether the widget ticks when offscreen or not

Functions.Name: SetTickWhenOffscreen
Functions.Description: Sets whether the widget ticks when offscreen or not

Functions.Name: SetBackgroundColor
Functions.Description: Sets the background color and opacityscale for this widget

Functions.Name: SetTintColorAndOpacity
Functions.Description: Sets the tint color and opacity scale for this widget
